page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated physical cores running at a base clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.c7se.4xlarge in the average performance tier across most standard workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1591.0 and a multi-core score of 9594.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 21143.74. In memory-intensive tasks, the instance delivers a read-only small-block cached memory bandwidth of 857.97 GB/sec, which drops to 101.79 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For specialized workloads, the CPU's instruction set supports AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, enabling a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 84.64 GB/sec and a Redis SET throughput of 15.94 million operations per second. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in small-scale LLM text generation, processing 266.95 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7se.4xlarge offers dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading, ensuring predictable performance for compute-bound tasks without resource contention. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a highly structured option for workloads requiring consistent processing power over memory capacity. This hardware profile is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate solid mid-range capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 21143.74 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 9594.0. It exhibits strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 266.95 tokens per second on a 135M model. This instance is designed for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations where dedicated CPU resources are required without the overhead of shared-core architectures.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for predictable CPU-bound workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 16-core Intel Xeon compute instance with a 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable, high-throughput processing workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
AVX-512 and cryptography hardware acceleration support
